title: Backyard Bear Break-In
description: A resident calls 911 about a large black bear rummaging through garbage and trying to enter the house through the back door.
initial_variations: There's a huge bear in my backyard! It just knocked over the trash cans and is heading for the house! Please send someone now!
I'm {caller_name}, and there's a bear outside my kitchen window! It's sniffing around the back door, I think it's trying to get in!
Oh my god, a bear! It just came out of the woods and is in my yard. I can hear {ambient_audio}. Help!
Emergency! A bear is on my property. It's pawing at the sliding glass door! I need help fast!

specific_details: it's a large black bear, maybe 300 pounds, with two cubs
it's a brown bear, drooling and huffing loudly
the bear has a visible ear tag and is limping
ambient_audio: heavy breathing and scratching at the door
a low, rumbling growl and breaking wood

initial: I'm at {address_location}. The bear is in the backyard, near the deck.
My address is {address_location}. It's right outside my back door!
We're at {address_location}. I'm in the kitchen, looking out the window.
bear_moving_closer: Still at {address_location}. The bear is now on the deck, pressing against the glass!
bear_left: I think it wandered off into the woods behind {address_location}, but I'm not sure.
bear_trapped: Animal control has the bear at {address_location}, we're safe now.

initial: {specific_details}. It's currently knocking over the grill.
I see {specific_details}. It just broke the bird feeder.
bear_moving_closer: It's {specific_details} and now it's trying to push open the sliding door!
The bear is {specific_details}. It's looking right at me through the glass.
bear_left: It left after eating all the trash. {specific_details}. I think it went towards the creek.
bear_trapped: Animal control darted it. {specific_details}. They're loading it into a truck now.

initial: I'm inside with the doors locked, but it's heading this way. I'm terrified!
bear_moving_closer: It's banging on the glass! I've moved to the living room. Please hurry!
bear_left: I'm still inside, shaking. I can't see it anymore.
bear_trapped: I'm fine now. The animal control officer said it's a mother bear with cubs.

initial: Okay, I'll stay inside and make sure all windows are locked.
bear_moving_closer: I'm not going out there! But it's right at the door...
bear_left: It seems to have wandered off now. I still hear rustling in the woods.
bear_trapped: Yes, I stayed inside the whole time. Thank goodness for animal control.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive and secure the area, but without tranquilizers the bear remains a threat. They request animal control backup.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters arrive with a hose but only agitate the bear, causing it to break a window before retreating.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics stand by but cannot handle the animal; the bear continues to rampage.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control officers use a tranquilizer dart to safely sedate the bear and relocate it with its cubs.

DISMISS.message: The bear breaks into the house, causing thousands in damages and terrorizing the family until a neighbor calls 911 again.

title: Poolside Alligator Intruder
description: A homeowner discovers a large alligator in their swimming pool while their dog is dangerously close, barking at it.
initial_variations: Help! There's an alligator in my pool! My dog is outside and won't stop barking at it. I think it's going to eat him!
I'm {caller_name}. I just looked outside and there's a massive alligator floating in my swimming pool. My {victim_relation} is out there! Hurry!
Oh no! A huge alligator just climbed the fence and slid into my pool. I can hear {ambient_audio}. Send help now!
There's a gator in my backyard pool! It's enormous and my little terrier is going crazy at the edge. I'm afraid it'll lunge out!

specific_details: the gator is at least 8 feet long, with a thick snout
it's sunning itself on the pool steps, mouth slightly open
the alligator is missing a foot and has scars on its back
ambient_audio: a low hissing and dog whimpering

initial: I'm at {address_location}. The pool is in the screened lanai.
My house is {address_location}. The gator is in the deep end!
We're at {address_location}. I can see it from the patio door.
dog_in_danger: Still at {address_location}. My {victim_relation} is right at the pool edge, barking!
alligator_moved: The gator crawled out of the pool at {address_location} and is now sunning on the deck!
situation_controlled: Animal control has it contained at {address_location}, they're taking it away.

initial: {specific_details}. It's just floating there, barely moving.
I see {specific_details}. It splashed when it first got in.
dog_in_danger: {specific_details}. Its tail just twitched—I think it's watching the dog!
The gator is {specific_details}. It's starting to swim toward the steps where my {victim_relation} is!
alligator_moved: {specific_details}. It's now resting right next to the patio furniture.
situation_controlled: They snared it. {specific_details}. It's in a trailer now.

initial: I'm inside with the door closed, but my dog is outside and I can't get him to come in.
dog_in_danger: I'm freaking out! The gator is moving toward my {victim_relation}! I want to run out but I'm scared.
alligator_moved: I opened the door and my dog ran inside. Now we're both safe, but the gator is on land!
situation_controlled: We're both okay. The dog is shaking but unharmed. That was too close.

initial: I can't get him to come in! He thinks it's a game.
dog_in_danger: I tried calling him, but he's too focused. Wait—the gator just flipped its tail! I'm going to grab him!
alligator_moved: I grabbed my {victim_relation} and shut the door. The gator is now out of the pool and on the deck.
situation_controlled: He's safe now, curled up on the couch. No more gators, please.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive and evacuate the family but lack tools to remove the alligator, which remains a hazard.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters attempt to spray the alligator to shoo it away, but it snaps and charges, forcing them to retreat.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics arrive but can't extract the alligator; the dog is eventually bitten before animal control arrives.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control officers use a catchpole and a board to safely secure the alligator, releasing it into a nearby swamp.

DISMISS.message: The alligator kills the dog and enters the house through the dog door, trapping the family until a neighbor intervenes.

title: Wild Turkey Road Rampage
description: A driver is trapped inside their car on a rural road as an aggressive flock of wild turkeys surrounds and attacks their vehicle.
initial_variations: I'm stuck on a road and there's a huge flock of turkeys! They're surrounding my car and pecking at the tires. I can't move!
Help! I'm {caller_name}. There's a gang of turkeys blocking the road and they're attacking my vehicle! I'm near a farm, but I can't see a name.
There are like 30 turkeys in the middle of the road and they won't budge. One just flew onto my hood! I'm scared they'll break my windshield.
I'm being attacked by wild turkeys! They're all around my car, making {ambient_audio}. I can't drive without running them over!

address_location: Old Mill Road near the red barn
County Highway W, 2 miles east of town
specific_details: the turkeys are huge, with red wattles, and there are over 40 of them
they are pecking at the car's paint and one is on the roof
it looks like a mix of hens and toms, and the toms are fluffed up and charging
ambient_audio: loud gobbling and thumping on metal
a cacophony of gobbles and my car alarm going off

initial: I'm on {address_location}. There are woods on both sides.
I'm at {address_location}. I was just driving when they swarmed me.
My GPS says {address_location}. I'm the only car here.
turkeys_aggressive: Still on {address_location}. They're not letting up—now they're jumping on the hood!
turkeys_dispersed: The animal control truck is here at {address_location}. They're using brooms to shoo them away.
road_clear: I'm at {address_location}. The road is clear now, I can leave.

initial: {specific_details}. They came out of the woods suddenly.
I see {specific_details}. It's like a turkey tornado!
turkeys_aggressive: {specific_details}. One just flew at my window! I think it saw its reflection.
They're {specific_details}. I'm afraid they'll damage the car.
turkeys_dispersed: They're retreating into the woods now. {specific_details}. The officers are herding them with nets.
road_clear: The turkeys are gone. {specific_details} were no joke. Thanks!

initial: I'm locked in my car. They started out just in the road, but now they're coming closer.
turkeys_aggressive: It's getting worse! They're pecking at the windows and I can hear the paint scratching. Please hurry!
turkeys_dispersed: I'm still in the car, but the turkeys are backing off. I see a truck with a state logo.
road_clear: I'm fine, just a few scratches on the car. That was wild.

initial: Okay, I'll stay put. But they're starting to surround me.
turkeys_aggressive: I'm definitely not getting out! They look mean. Oh, I see lights—it might be animal control!
turkeys_dispersed: I stayed in the car. The officers are shooing the last few away.
road_clear: Yes, I'm still inside. They're all gone now. I can continue my trip.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive and try to disperse the turkeys with sirens, but the birds become more agitated and attack the officers' vehicle.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters use water hoses, which scatter some turkeys but cause the rest to fly onto nearby trees, only to return minutes later.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics arrive but cannot address the bird situation; the turkeys continue their assault for another hour.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control officers use turkey calls and herding techniques to safely guide the flock back into the woods, clearing the road.

DISMISS.message: The turkeys eventually puncture a tire and break a window, causing thousands in damage and leaving the driver stranded for hours.

title: Moose on the Loose Downtown
description: A moose wanders into a busy downtown area, stopping traffic, charging at pedestrians, and causing widespread panic.
initial_variations: There's a moose walking down Main Street! It's huge and it's heading towards the park. People are running!
I'm {caller_name}. A moose just came out of the woods and is on the loose downtown. I'm watching from a window, and it's starting to act aggressive, snorting and pawing the ground.
Oh my god, a moose is charging at cars! It just rammed a sedan! I can hear {ambient_audio}. Send help!
Emergency! A moose is on the loose in the city center. It's already kicked a car and is blocking traffic. We need animal control immediately!

specific_details: it's a bull moose with huge antlers, at least 7 feet tall
the moose has a broken antler and seems injured, limping slightly
it's a cow moose with two calves trailing behind her
ambient_audio: a loud bellow and shattering glass

initial: It's at {address_location}. It came from the north side.
We're at {address_location}. It's right in the middle of the intersection!
I'm calling from {address_location}. The moose is outside the coffee shop.
moose_agitated: Still at {address_location}. It just charged a bus and then backed off.
moose_contained: They've cornered it near {address_location}, by the library.
all_clear: The area around {address_location} is safe now. The moose has been tranquilized.

initial: {specific_details}. It's walking slowly but seems uneasy.
I see {specific_details}. People are trying to take photos—that's dangerous!
moose_agitated: {specific_details}. It's snorting and stomping, and it just knocked over a newsstand.
The moose is {specific_details}. It's eyeing people who are too close. This is bad.
moose_contained: {specific_details}. Animal control has it surrounded with temporary fencing.
all_clear: The moose is down. {specific_details}. They're loading it onto a flatbed to relocate.

initial: Some people are hiding in shops, but a few idiots are getting close to take pictures!
moose_agitated: It just charged a cyclist! People are screaming and running everywhere.
moose_contained: The police have set up a perimeter. No one is near it now.
all_clear: Everyone is staying back. The area is taped off.

initial: I'll try to tell people, but they aren't listening. The moose is getting agitated.
moose_agitated: I'm shouting from the window, but it's chaos. Wait—I see animal control arriving!
moose_contained: They're clearing the area now with bullhorns. The moose is boxed in.
all_clear: Yes, the area is clear. The tranquilizer took effect.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police close streets and evacuate the area, but they lack sedatives, so the moose remains a threat until animal control arrives.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters use a ladder truck to shield people, but the moose charges the truck and injures a firefighter.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics treat injuries but cannot control the moose, resulting in more casualties.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control experts use a dart rifle to tranquilize the moose from a safe distance, then relocate it to a remote forest.

DISMISS.message: The moose rampages through the downtown, injuring a dozen people and causing massive property damage before disappearing into a park.

title: Schoolyard Bee Invasion
description: A school playground is swarmed by aggressive bees after a hive falls, and children are being stung with some potentially allergic.
initial_variations: Help! Bees! There's a giant swarm of bees on the school playground and the kids are getting stung! We need help now!
I'm {caller_name}, the principal. A massive beehive fell from a tree and thousands of bees are attacking the children. Some are having allergic reactions!
There are bees everywhere at the elementary school! They're swarming the kids. I can hear {ambient_audio}. Please send paramedics and someone to remove the bees!
Emergency! A swarm of bees has taken over the playground. Children are trapped and being stung. We need animal control and ambulances!

specific_details: the hive was the size of a basketball and it fell near the slide
it's a swarm of Africanized honeybees, extremely aggressive
there are multiple hives in the old oak tree, and one branch broke
ambient_audio: panicked shouting and cries of pain
a low roar of bees and the school alarm ringing

initial: We're at {address_location}. The playground is behind the main building.
This is {address_location}. The bees are between the jungle gym and the swings.
I'm at {address_location}. The hive fell on the north side of the playground.
children_in_danger: Still at {address_location}. We can't get to the kids! They're covered in bees.
area_sealed: We've locked the doors at {address_location}, but the bees are still outside.
bees_removed: The beekeeper is here at {address_location}. He's removing the hive now.

initial: {specific_details}. A few kids already have stings all over their arms.
I see {specific_details}. The bees are thick in the air—you can barely see.
children_in_danger: {specific_details}. Two children are down on the ground, not moving. I think they're having reactions!
It's {specific_details}. The bees are getting into the classrooms through open windows.
area_sealed: {specific_details}. We've secured the building, but the hive is still active.
bees_removed: The hive has been removed. {specific_details}. The bees are calming down now.

initial: Several kids have been stung multiple times. One child has a history of allergies and I can't find their EpiPen!
children_in_danger: It's getting worse! A teacher is also down, and we need ambulances. The bees are still attacking.
area_sealed: We have the kids inside, but many are crying in pain. The school nurse is out sick today.
bees_removed: Paramedics are treating the stings. The child with the allergy is stable now.

initial: I'm telling teachers to close windows, but the kids are outside for recess!
children_in_danger: We're trying to bring them inside, but the bees are so aggressive. Wait—the custodian is using a hose to create a mist so we can run through!
area_sealed: All children are inside now. The playground is empty, but the bees are still there.
bees_removed: We sheltered inside until the beekeeper finished. The children are safe.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police evacuate the area but cannot remove the bees; the hive remains a threat for hours until a beekeeper is found.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters use foam to suppress the bees, but it only angers them further, causing more stings before they retreat.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics treat the injured, but without removal of the bees, more children are stung as they exit the school.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control dispatches a specialist beekeeper who safely removes the hive and relocates the bees, ending the threat.

DISMISS.message: The bees swarm multiple classrooms, leading to dozens of stings and two severe allergic reactions that become life-threatening.

title: Bear in Suburban Backyard
description: A homeowner attempts to break into their house through the back door.
initial_variations: {caller_name} here, there's a {animal_description} in my backyard! It's trying to get inside!
Help! A {animal_description} is clawing at my back door. I think it's going to break in!
Oh my god, a {animal_description} is on my property and it's breaking into the house! Please send someone!
Emergency! We have a {animal_description} in our backyard! My name is {caller_name}, it's getting aggressive!

specific_details: the back door is splintering
I can see its claws through the crack
it's a grizzly with a cub

initial: I'm at {address_location}. The bear is in the backyard!
bear_attempting_entry: Still at {address_location}. It's trying to smash the back door!
bear_deterred: At {address_location}. I made noise and it backed off a bit.
animal_secured: Animal control is here at {address_location}. They've contained the bear.

initial: I was inside and heard a crash. I looked out and saw a {animal_description} at the back door. {specific_details}.
bear_attempting_entry: It's a {animal_description}. {specific_details}. It's trying to force the door open.
bear_deterred: It's still in the yard, pacing. {specific_details}. It's growling but not attacking right now.
animal_secured: The bear has been tranquilized and taken away. No one hurt.

initial: It's just standing there, but it's huge. I'm afraid it'll charge.
bear_attempting_entry: It's slamming against the door! I hear wood splintering!
bear_deterred: I banged pots together and it backed off to the fence. It's still watching.
animal_secured: Animal control has it tranquilized. They're loading it onto a truck.

initial: No one is hurt, but I'm scared the bear will get inside.
bear_attempting_entry: No injuries yet, but if it breaks the door, we might be in trouble!
bear_deterred: Everyone is inside and safe for now.
animal_secured: No injuries reported. The bear is secured.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive but are not equipped to handle the bear; they call animal control.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters arrive but lack animal control training; they secure the perimeter.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Medics arrive but there are no injuries; they stand by.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control officers safely tranquilize and relocate the bear.

DISMISS.message: The call is dismissed as a prank; the bear breaks in and causes injuries.

title: Bull on the Loose
description: A bull has escaped and is rampaging through a residential street, charging at people and cars.
initial_variations: {caller_name} calling! There's a {animal_description} loose on our street! It's charging at everything!
Help! A {animal_description} just ran down my block. It's going crazy!
Emergency! I'm {caller_name}, and there's a {animal_description} running wild in the neighborhood!
Oh no, a {animal_description} is attacking cars outside. Please send someone!

specific_details: it just gored a mailbox
it's flipping over trash cans
it charged a dog walker

initial: We're at {address_location}. The bull is in the middle of the street!
bull_rampaging: Still at {address_location}. It's smashing against cars now!
bull_contained: At {address_location}. Some folks managed to fence it into a yard.
animal_secured: Animal control has it corralled at {address_location}.

initial: It's a {animal_description}. {specific_details}. It's completely out of control.
bull_rampaging: {specific_details}. The bull is snorting and pawing the ground.
bull_contained: {specific_details}. It's trapped but still very agitated.
animal_secured: The bull is secured in a trailer. No injuries.

initial: It's running up and down the street. I've never seen anything like this.
bull_rampaging: It just charged a van! The driver is stuck inside!
bull_contained: Neighbors used garbage bins to block the alleyway. It can't get out.
animal_secured: They've got it in a trailer. Crisis averted.

initial: No one's hurt yet, but it's only a matter of time.
bull_rampaging: A man fell while running, but he's okay. Just scrapes.
bull_contained: Everyone's staying inside now. No injuries.
animal_secured: No injuries reported. The bull is safely contained.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive and try to redirect traffic but cannot capture the bull; they wait for animal control.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters assist with barriers but lack animal capture equipment.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Medics arrive to treat minor injuries but cannot handle the animal.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control officers use a tranquilizer and successfully capture the bull.

DISMISS.message: Call is dismissed; the bull causes property damage and injures a pedestrian.

title: Raccoon Invasion in Attic
description: A family reports a family of raccoons has taken over their attic, causing damage and one is acting aggressively.
initial_variations: Hi, I'm {caller_name}. We have a serious problem: there are {animal_description} in our attic!
Help! {animal_description} are tearing up our attic! They sound huge and aggressive.
This is {caller_name}. I can hear {animal_description} above me. It's getting louder!
Emergency! We've got {animal_description} in the house! They're in the attic and they sound dangerous.

specific_details: they've torn open a vent
I see a mother with babies
they've chewed electrical wires

initial: We're at {address_location}. The raccoons are in the attic.
raccoon_scurrying: Still at {address_location}. They're right above my head now.
raccoon_charge: {address_location}! One just lunged from the attic opening!
animal_secured: Animal control is here at {address_location}. They've captured the raccoons.

initial: I was in the living room and heard scratching. I peeked into the attic and saw {animal_description}. {specific_details}.
raccoon_scurrying: They're running back and forth. {specific_details}. It's chaos up there.
raccoon_charge: One raccoon bared its teeth and charged at us when we tried to close the hatch.
animal_secured: They trapped all the raccoons. The attic will need repairs, but we're safe.

initial: I can hear them moving around. It sounds like they're fighting.
raccoon_scurrying: They're getting more agitated. I hear growling now!
raccoon_charge: We slammed the attic door shut. One raccoon keeps trying to push it open.
animal_secured: Animal control set traps and removed them all. They'll be relocated.

initial: No one is hurt, but we're scared to go near the attic.
raccoon_scurrying: My son is crying, but no injuries.
raccoon_charge: My husband got scratched trying to shut the hatch. It's minor.
animal_secured: Just the scratch, already cleaned and bandaged.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive but cannot remove wildlife; they call animal control.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters help secure the attic access but wait for animal control.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Medics treat a minor scratch, but the primary issue requires animal removal.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control officers safely trap and remove the raccoons, advising on attic repairs.

DISMISS.message: Call dismissed as nuisance; raccoons cause electrical fire later that night.

title: Swan Blocking Traffic
description: An aggressive swan is holding up traffic at a busy intersection, attacking vehicles and refusing to move.
initial_variations: {caller_name} here. There's a {animal_description} in the middle of the intersection! It's blocking all the cars!
Help! A {animal_description} is attacking cars at the corner of Main and Third! This is {caller_name}.
Emergency! I'm {caller_name}. A {animal_description} won't let anyone pass. It's pecking at tires!
You won't believe this: a {animal_description} is causing a traffic jam. It's furious!

specific_details: it's hissing and flapping its wings
it's pecking at car tires
it just attacked a cyclist

initial: It's at {address_location}. The swan is right in the middle of the road.
swan_attacking: Still at {address_location}. It's attacking cars in all directions.
swan_deterred: We managed to shoo it to the side of the road at {address_location}.
animal_secured: Animal control has it contained on the median at {address_location}.

initial: It's a {animal_description}. {specific_details}. Traffic is completely stopped.
swan_attacking: {specific_details}. It's extremely territorial and won't let anyone near.
swan_deterred: It's pacing on the shoulder. Still hissing at anyone who gets close.
animal_secured: The swan is in a crate. Roads are clearing.

initial: It's just standing there, but whenever a car tries to inch forward, it charges.
swan_attacking: It's flapping its wings and bashing against a truck's door! This is insane.
swan_deterred: We used a jacket to herd it away from the center. It's still aggressive, though.
animal_secured: An officer netted it. It's safe and no one was hurt.

initial: No injuries, but everyone is stuck in their cars.
swan_attacking: A man on a bike got a peck on the leg, but it's just a bruise.
swan_deterred: No serious injuries. A few scared kids in cars.
animal_secured: No injuries except the cyclist with a minor bruise.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive and direct traffic but cannot capture the swan; they wait for animal control.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters assist with traffic barriers but lack animal handling expertise.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Medics respond but only treat a minor bruise; the swan remains a hazard.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control officers safely capture the swan and release it in a nearby lake.

DISMISS.message: Call is dismissed; the swan causes a multi-car fender bender and injures a child.

title: Poisonous Snake in Classroom
description: A teacher calls 911 after discovering a venomous snake in a classroom with students present.
initial_variations: This is {caller_name} at the elementary school. There's a {animal_description} in my classroom!
Help! I'm {caller_name}, a teacher. We've just found a {animal_description} under a desk. Children are here!
Emergency! We need animal control! A {animal_description} is in the schoolroom. My name is {caller_name}.
Oh my god, a {animal_description} is in the classroom! I'm {caller_name}. I don't know what to do!

specific_details: it's a rattlesnake with a diamond pattern
it's coiled and rattling
it's an escaped cobra from the science lab

initial: We're at {address_location}. The snake is in Room 12.
snake_spotted: Still at {address_location}. The snake hasn't moved from under the desk.
children_evacuated: Children are out, but the snake is still in the classroom at {address_location}.
animal_secured: Animal control is here at {address_location}. They've captured the snake.

initial: I saw something move near the bookshelf. It's a {animal_description}. {specific_details}.
snake_spotted: {specific_details}. The children haven't noticed it yet, but I'm terrified.
children_evacuated: The snake is still coiled. {specific_details}. It rattled when the last child left.
animal_secured: It was a {animal_description}. {specific_details}. They used a snake hook and bagged it.

initial: I just saw it. I'm trying to keep the children calm and away.
snake_spotted: It's coiled up and rattling. I think it's about to strike.
children_evacuated: We got all 25 kids out silently. The snake is still there.
animal_secured: A herpetologist from animal control has safely removed it.

initial: No one is bitten. I'm keeping everyone at the opposite end of the room.
snake_spotted: Still no bites. But a child almost stepped on it.
animal_secured: No injuries at all. The school is safe.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police help evacuate the school but cannot handle the venomous snake; they call animal control.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters assist with evacuation but lack snake-handling expertise.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Medics stand by for potential snakebites but cannot address the snake itself.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control's venomous snake specialist safely captures and removes the snake.

DISMISS.message: Call is dismissed as a prank; the snake bites a child, causing a medical emergency.

title: Elephant Escapes Circus, Causes Highway Chaos
description: A caller reports an escaped circus elephant blocking a major highway, causing traffic hazards and potential danger to motorists.
initial_variations: Oh my god, there's an elephant on the freeway! It's huge and it's just standing there! This is {caller_name}, I'm in my car and I can't move!
Help! A circus elephant named {elephant_name} has escaped and is blocking the highway! I'm {caller_name} and I'm stuck in traffic. Send someone quickly!
There's a full-sized elephant just walking down the interstate! It's {elephant_name} from {circus_name}! My name is {caller_name} and I need help. It's going to cause an accident!

specific_details: It's swaying back and forth, seems agitated
It's using its trunk to nudge cars

initial: I'm on {address_location}, the elephant is right in the middle of the road!
It's right here on {address_location}, traffic is backed up for miles.
at_safe_distance: The incident is still at {address_location}, but I've pulled over safely about 200 yards back.
I'm on the shoulder of {address_location}, the elephant hasn't moved. I can see it from here.
animal_contained: The elephant is contained, but we're still on {address_location}, waiting for the road to clear.
We're at {address_location}, they're loading the elephant onto a truck now.

initial: It's a huge African elephant, maybe 10 feet tall. {ambient_audio}. {specific_details}.
He's got a circus blanket on, looks agitated. {ambient_audio}. {specific_details}.
at_safe_distance: I can see it from here, it's pacing and swinging its trunk. {ambient_audio}.
It's still {specific_details}. Some people are getting out of their cars, but it's dangerous.
animal_contained: They've got it tranquilized and are loading it onto a truck. The road is still blocked though.
The elephant is sedated and secured. Traffic is starting to move slowly.

initial: I'm parked behind it, but I'm scared. I'm going to back up and move to the shoulder.
I'm trying to keep my distance. I'm moving my car away now.
at_safe_distance: I'm safe now, I've moved my car to the shoulder about 100 yards back.
Yes, I'm at a safe distance. I can still see the elephant.
animal_contained: I'm safe, and it looks like the elephant is under control now.
I'm fine, they have it contained.

initial: It's still just standing there, no change. When are you sending someone?
Nothing has changed, the elephant is still blocking the road.
at_safe_distance: They're here! Animal control just arrived. I see them trying to corral the elephant.
I see animal control with a capture team. They're approaching the elephant.
animal_contained: Yes, they have it secured now. The road is still blocked, but the elephant is safe.
It's under control. They're giving it a tranquilizer.

initial: I think it's from the circus that's in town, {circus_name}. The elephant's name is {elephant_name}.
It's {elephant_name} from {circus_name}. I heard they were set up at the fairgrounds.
at_safe_distance: Yeah, definitely from {circus_name}. I saw their trucks earlier.
It's {elephant_name}, the circus elephant. They might have lost it during a show.
animal_contained: They've got handlers from the circus here now helping. It's {elephant_name} from {circus_name}.
Circus staff arrived. It's their elephant, {elephant_name}.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive and block traffic, but they need animal control to handle the elephant. Delay in resolution.

SEND_FIRE.message: Fire Department responds with a heavy rescue unit, but they lack animal training. They assist with traffic control.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Medics arrive, but there are no injuries. They wait for animal control.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal Control arrives with specialized capture team, safely tranquilizing the elephant and returning it to the circus.

DISMISS.message: The call is dismissed as a prank, leading to a major traffic accident when the elephant panics.

title: Swarm of Bats Invades Hospital Emergency Room
description: A hospital administrator calls 911 because a huge swarm of bats has flooded the emergency room, causing chaos and potential health risks.
initial_variations: This is {caller_name}, we have a massive bat infestation in the emergency room! They're swarming everywhere, please send someone!
Emergency! Hundreds of bats just flew into the ER! I'm {caller_name}, we need animal control immediately!
Help! Bats are everywhere in the hospital ER! This is {caller_name}, they came from the vents and people are panicking!

specific_details: They came through the ventilation shaft
They're clinging to the ceiling and swooping at patients

initial: We're at {address_location}, in the emergency department.
It's the ER at {address_location}. Please hurry!
patients_evacuated: We evacuated patients to another wing, but the bats are still at {address_location} in the ER.
The affected area is the ER at {address_location}. The rest of the hospital is clear.
bats_removed: The ER at {address_location} is being sanitized now, the bats are gone.
We're at {address_location}, the situation is under control.

initial: {specific_details}. {ambient_audio}. It's chaos, we can't treat patients!
There are hundreds of them, {specific_details}. {ambient_audio}.
patients_evacuated: The bats are still flying around. {ambient_audio}. We sealed off the ER.
They're agitated and aggressive. {ambient_audio}. We're keeping everyone out.
bats_removed: Animal control used nets and smoke to remove them. The ER is a mess but they're out.
They captured most of them. A few stragglers might still be in the vents.

initial: We're evacuating the ER now. Patients are being moved to the hallways.
I'm safe, but we're moving all patients out of the ER immediately.
patients_evacuated: All patients and staff are out of the ER. We're in a safe area now.
Everyone is safe, we locked the ER doors to contain the bats.
bats_removed: We're all safe. The ER is being cleaned.
Yes, the situation is under control.

initial: Nothing yet, we're still waiting for help!
The bats are still everywhere. We're getting more panicked calls.
patients_evacuated: Animal control is here! They're setting up nets and trying to smoke them out.
I see animal control with protective gear. They're entering the ER.
bats_removed: Yes, they've cleared almost all of them. It's finally quiet.
They're removing the last ones. The ER will need deep cleaning.

initial: They came from the ventilation system. We think there's a roost in the attic.
They must have gotten in through an open vent on the roof. I heard them before they came down.
patients_evacuated: We traced it to a broken vent grille in the ceiling. They were nesting in the ductwork.
Maintenance says there's a colony in the old boiler room vent.
bats_removed: The animal control team found the entry point on the roof and sealed it.
They identified the roost and are removing it to prevent another infestation.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police secure the area and help with crowd control, but they can't remove the bats. Animal control still needed.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters assist with evacuation and ventilation, but they lack expertise for bat removal.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Additional medics arrive to treat potential bat bites, but no one is seriously hurt. The bats remain.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal Control arrives with bat-handling equipment, safely removes the swarm, and seals the entry point.

DISMISS.message: The call is dismissed as an exaggeration, leading to a patient being bitten and the ER shutting down due to health code violations.

title: Aggressive Peacock Harasses Joggers in Park
description: Caller reports an aggressive peacock attacking joggers and cyclists in a public park, causing minor injuries and panic.
initial_variations: Help! There's a crazy peacock attacking people in the park! This is {caller_name}, I just got chased!
A peacock is going nuts! It's aggressive and attacking anyone who comes near. {caller_name} here, send animal control!
We're at the park and this peacock won't stop attacking joggers! I'm {caller_name}, someone needs to catch it!

specific_details: It's chasing anyone in bright clothing
It has a large tail span and keeps attacking cyclists

initial: We're at {address_location}, the peacock is right on the path.
It's happening at {address_location}. There's a crowd forming.
safe_distance: I'm watching from the gazebo at {address_location}. The peacock is still chasing people near the fountain.
The peacock hasn't moved from {address_location}. I'm at a safe spot by the benches.
peacock_captured: They caught it near the rose garden at {address_location}. It's in a crate now.
We're at {address_location}. The animal control officer has it under control.

initial: {specific_details}. {ambient_audio}. It's scratched a couple of people already!
It's a full-grown male, blue and green. {ambient_audio}. {specific_details}.
safe_distance: It's still aggressive. {ambient_audio}. It just knocked a bike over!
I can see it fanning its tail and charging at anyone who gets close. {ambient_audio}.
peacock_captured: They got it with a net. It's quiet now.
It's in a carrying case, but still squawking a bit.

initial: I climbed onto a picnic table to get away. I'm moving back to the parking lot.
I'm jogging back to my car. I'm not hurt, but others might be.
safe_distance: I'm safe now, I'm in my car with the doors locked.
Yes, I'm far enough away. The peacock can't get me here.
peacock_captured: I'm safe, they have the bird.

initial: It's still going crazy! Please hurry!
Nothing yet, it's still terrorizing the park.
safe_distance: Animal control just pulled up! They've got nets and a cage.
I see an animal control officer approaching the peacock. It's not happy.
peacock_captured: Yes, they caught it! It's in a crate now. Thank you!
They've got it secured. The park can go back to normal.

initial: I think it's a wild peacock that lives in the park. There's a flock here, but this one is aggressive.
Some people feed them, but this one is just mean. I don't think it belongs to anyone.
safe_distance: It's definitely one of the feral peacocks. They nest in the woods here.
No idea, it might be from a nearby estate. It's been here for years.
peacock_captured: Animal control said it's a feral peacock, they're taking it to a sanctuary.
They suspect it's a pet that got loose. It's too aggressive for a wild bird.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police cordon off the area to prevent injuries, but still need animal control to capture the bird.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters arrive but lack equipment for bird capture; they standby until animal control arrives.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Medics treat minor scratches, but the peacock remains a menace until animal control arrives.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal Control safely nets and removes the aggressive peacock, relocating it to a wildlife center.

DISMISS.message: The call is dismissed as a nuisance, resulting in multiple serious injuries as the peacock continues attacking.

title: Venomous Tarantula Found in Daycare Center
description: A daycare worker calls 911 after discovering a large venomous tarantula in the play area, children present.
initial_variations: There's a giant spider in the daycare! A tarantula! This is {caller_name}, we need someone now!
Help! We found a huge, hairy spider in the toy bin! I'm {caller_name} from the daycare, the kids are terrified!
Emergency! A tarantula is loose in our daycare center! {caller_name} calling, send animal control immediately!

specific_details: It's in the toy bin, red knees and about six inches across
It's crawling on the carpet near the nap mats, very fast

initial: We're at {address_location}, in the toddler playroom.
It's at {address_location}. Please send help, the kids are scared.
children_safe: The kids are in the hallway of {address_location}. The spider is still in the playroom.
We're all at {address_location} but in a separate room. The spider hasn't moved.
spider_captured: The spider is gone from {address_location}. Playroom is being checked.
We're still at {address_location}, but it's safe now.

initial: {specific_details}. {ambient_audio}. We're afraid it might bite a child!
It's a Chilean rose tarantula maybe, {specific_details}. {ambient_audio}.
children_safe: From the window, I can see it's climbed onto a bookshelf. {ambient_audio} is quieter now.
It's hiding under a chair now. The room is quiet, but we're keeping the door shut.
spider_captured: Animal control caught it in a container. It was a tarantula, definitely an escaped pet.
They've got it. It's in a secure box.

initial: I'm getting all the kids out of the playroom now. We're moving to the hallway.
We're evacuating the room. I'm making sure no child is near it.
children_safe: All children and staff are out. We've closed the door.
Everyone is safe in the art room. No one was bitten.
spider_captured: We're all fine. The spider has been removed.
Safe now. The kids are calming down.

initial: Nothing yet! We're still panicking here!
It's still loose. We're keeping the kids away.
children_safe: I see an animal control van pulling in! They're coming inside.
Animal control is here! They're going into the playroom.
spider_captured: Yes, they caught it! It was in a little plastic tank now.
The spider is secured. They said it's venomous but not lethal to humans.

initial: We have no idea! It's not ours. Maybe a parent brought it or it escaped from somewhere.
It must be an escaped pet. No one here owns a tarantula.
children_safe: We checked with staff, no one claims it. It might have come in from outside or a backpack.
One child said her brother has spiders, but we can't confirm.
spider_captured: Animal control thinks it's a desert tarantula, likely a pet that got loose. They'll try to find the owner.
They said it's common in the pet trade. It might have been released accidentally.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police respond and secure the area, but they are not equipped to handle venomous spiders. Animal control is still needed.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters arrive but can only protect the area; they wait for animal control to safely remove the tarantula.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Medics check for bites, but no one is injured. The spider remains a threat.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal Control captures the tarantula safely and identifies it as a non-lethal species, returning it to its owner.

DISMISS.message: Dismissing the call leads to a child being bitten, causing severe allergic reaction and panic at the daycare.

title: Sea Lion Blocks Boat Ramp, Strands Fishermen
description: A sea lion has beached itself on a boat ramp, blocking access and stranding multiple fishing boats.
initial_variations: A huge sea lion is blocking the boat ramp! This is {caller_name}, we can't launch our boats!
Help! There's a massive sea lion on the ramp, and it won't move! I'm {caller_name}, we're stuck!
We've got a sea lion just lounging on the boat ramp, it's aggressive if you get close! {caller_name} here, send someone!

specific_details: It's a huge male, maybe 800 pounds, and it's growling at anyone who gets close
It's sunning itself right in the middle of the ramp, blocking two lanes

initial: We're at {address_location}. The ramp is completely blocked.
It's at {address_location}. Boats are lining up to get out.
area_cordoned: The ramp at {address_location} is taped off now, but the sea lion is still there.
We're waiting at {address_location}. The police set up a perimeter.
sea_lion_relocated: They moved it off the ramp! {address_location} is clear now.

initial: {specific_details}. {ambient_audio}. It's not moving at all.
It's a California sea lion, {specific_details}. {ambient_audio}.
area_cordoned: It's still there, barking and scratching itself. {ambient_audio}. It seems sick or injured maybe.
It's rolled onto its side now. {ambient_audio}. We're keeping distance.
sea_lion_relocated: Marine mammal rescue came and herded it onto a truck. It's being taken to a facility.
They used a special sling to move it. It's safe now.

initial: We're staying away from it, but it's right on the ramp. I'm telling everyone to back off.
I'm safe, I'm on my boat. But it's blocking us from docking too.
area_cordoned: The police advised us to stay back, so we're all on our boats or in the parking lot.
We're all safe. They won't let anyone near the ramp.
sea_lion_relocated: We're safe now, the area is clear.
All good, no one was hurt.

initial: Nothing, it's still just lying there. Getting impatient.
Still blocked. We need marine mammal rescue or something.
area_cordoned: I see a truck with marine mammal rescue arriving! They've got equipment.
Rescuers just got here. They're setting up barriers.
sea_lion_relocated: Yes, they coaxed it into a cage with fish. The ramp is open!
They successfully moved it. Boats are going again.

initial: No one's hurt, but a guy tried to shoo it and almost got bitten.
Not yet, but if it charges someone, it could be bad.
area_cordoned: No injuries. Everyone kept their distance.
Still no injuries. Police are making sure.
sea_lion_relocated: No one hurt. Rescuers said the sea lion was malnourished, it might have just been resting.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police secure the area and manage crowds, but marine mammal rescue is still required to move the sea lion.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters can hose the sea lion to encourage it to move, but it's stressed and could become aggressive. Animal control is better suited.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Medics stand by in case of injuries, but they cannot move the animal.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal Control coordinates with marine mammal rescue to safely relocate the sea lion to a rehabilitation center.

DISMISS.message: The call is dismissed as a low-priority nuisance. The sea lion later charges a child, causing serious injury.
